signals.signal_title: CloudBurst Data Centers natural gas supply — 1.2 GW AI data center, San Marcos TX
signals.signal_description: CloudBurst Data Centers long-term natural gas supply agreement — Oasis Pipeline to supply up to 450,000 MMBtu/day for ~1.2 GW AI data center in San Marcos TX. 10+ year term. Behind-the-meter power for AI compute campus.

signals.signal_title: Lake Charles LNG MidOcean JV — 30% stake, $3.5B commitment
signals.signal_description: Lake Charles LNG MidOcean JV — MidOcean commits 30% of construction costs and 30% of LNG production. 20-year Chevron SPA already in place. Large-scale LNG facility drives OT/SCADA and digital operations IT investment.

enrichment_notes: One of the largest US midstream MLPs — crude oil, NGL, natural gas pipelines, storage, terminals. Dallas HQ (STOLA, no channel mismatch). FY2025 Q4 Adjusted EBITDA $4.18B (+8%), full year ~$15.5B EBITDA. Revenue $92.3B (commodity pass-through heavy — midstream EBITDA×2.5 denominator applies). ~12,000 employees. No standalone CIO identified — operations-focused MLP structure. Co-CEO Tom Long drives AI data center power positioning strategy. Key signal: CloudBurst Data Centers long-term natural gas supply agreement signed Feb 2025 — up to 1.2 GW behind-the-meter power for AI data center in central Texas via Oasis Pipeline. Lake Charles LNG MidOcean JV (Apr 2025, 30% stake). Active expansion: Mustang Draw processing plant (275 MMcf/d, Permian, Q2 2026). No MSP confirmed. No M365 evidence. ai_maturity=5 — data center power positioning is energy revenue play not internal IT, no confirmed internal AI/digital programs. WWT angle: OT/SCADA for massive pipeline network, cybersecurity for critical infrastructure, data center connectivity infrastructure.
